concepts.title: Management is a Social Process
concepts.description: Management is a social process that involves influencing people to work together towards a common goal.

concepts.title: Management is a Process of Planning, Organizing, Staffing, Directing, Controlling
concepts.description: Management is a process of planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling the activities of an organization to achieve its objectives.

concepts.title: Management is both an Art and a Science
concepts.description: Management involves both art and science. It requires creative thinking and logical reasoning to solve problems and make decisions.

concepts.title: MOSDIC
concepts.description: Use the mnemonic 'MOSDIC' to remember the functions of management: M - Management, O - Organizing, S - Staffing, D - Directing, I - Inspiring, C - Controlling

concepts.title: Confusing Management with Administration
concepts.description: Management is not the same as administration. Management involves planning, organizing, and controlling, while administration involves implementing and executing the plans made by managers.

concepts.title: Management - Definition and Functions
concepts.description: The definition of management and its five functions: planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling are key concepts to remember in this chapter.

concepts.title: Management is a Continuous Process
concepts.description: Management is a continuous process that involves planning, organizing, staffing, directing, and controlling the activities of an organization to achieve its objectives.

concepts.title: Management is a Change Agent
concepts.description: Management is a change agent that helps organizations adapt to changing internal and external environments.
